issues.title: No @mention notification routing to actual project members
issues.description: MentionInput in src/components/activity/MentionInput.tsx and the activity feed have no integration with project_members table. Mentions produce no push notification, no email, and no in-app alert. A superintendent tagging the PM gets silence — a workflow-breaking gap versus Procore's ball-in-court model.
issues.prompt: In src/components/activity/MentionInput.tsx, wire the mention autocomplete to call getProjectMembers(projectId) from src/api/endpoints/projectMembers.ts and render a dropdown of real members (name, role, avatar). On mention selection, store the userId in the mention payload. In src/api/endpoints/activity.ts, after inserting an activity row, call a new function notifyMentionedUsers(mentionedUserIds, activityId, projectId) that inserts rows into a notifications table (columns: user_id, project_id, entity_type='activity', entity_id, type='mention', read=false, created_at). In src/stores/notificationStore.ts, subscribe to the notifications table via Supabase realtime for the current user and push a toast via the existing notification center. Acceptance criteria: (1) Typing @ in MentionInput shows real project member names from DB, (2) Selecting a member adds their userId to the mention list, (3) After submit, a notification row is created for each mentioned user, (4) The mentioned user sees a toast in their session within 2 seconds via realtime, (5) Notification count badge increments in TopBar.

issues.title: EditConflictGuard has no backend lock — race condition on concurrent edits
issues.description: src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx shows a UI lock banner but there is no server-side lock record. Two PMs can open the same RFI simultaneously, both see the banner as unlocked, and both save — last write wins with no conflict resolution. Data loss is guaranteed under concurrent use.
issues.prompt: In src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, on component mount call a new API function acquireEditLock(entityType, entityId, userId) in src/api/endpoints/editLocks.ts that upserts a row in an edit_locks table (entity_type, entity_id, locked_by_user_id, locked_at, expires_at = now+5min). If the row already exists and locked_by_user_id !== currentUser, return {locked: true, lockedBy}. Renew the lock every 60 seconds via setInterval. On unmount call releaseEditLock(entityType, entityId, userId). Subscribe to the edit_locks table via Supabase realtime filtered by entity_id so all sessions see lock state in real time. In src/lib/conflictResolver.ts, implement a three-way merge that compares before_state, server_state, and local_state — if conflict detected, open src/components/ui/ConflictResolutionModal.tsx with a side-by-side diff. Acceptance criteria: (1) Only one user can hold an active lock per entity at a time, (2) Second user sees a banner with the locking user's name and a request-access button, (3) Lock auto-expires after 5 minutes of inactivity, (4) Conflict modal appears when a stale edit is submitted against a changed server record.

issues.title: AI has no awareness of collaboration context — cannot surface team blockers
issues.description: The AI Copilot in src/pages/AICopilot.tsx and src/hooks/useProjectAI.ts does not consume presence data, unread mentions, or pending ball-in-court items. It cannot answer 'Who is blocking the curtain wall RFI?' or 'Which team members have not responded to the concrete pour meeting?' — core superintendent use cases the product vision demands.
issues.prompt: In src/lib/aiService.ts, extend the buildProjectContext function to include collaboration context: (1) fetch current presence from src/stores/presenceStore.ts (who is online), (2) fetch unread notifications from notificationStore, (3) fetch RFIs and submittals where ball_in_court matches a team member who has been idle >48h from activity_feed. Expose this as a collaborationContext object in the AIContext type in src/types/ai.ts. In src/lib/aiPrompts.ts, add a new prompt template COLLABORATION_BLOCKER_PROMPT that uses this context to answer 'Who is blocking X?' and 'Which items are waiting on [person]?'. In src/pages/AICopilot.tsx, add suggested prompts on the collaboration context page: 'Who is blocking the most open items?', 'Which team members have pending ball-in-court items?'. Acceptance criteria: (1) AIContext type includes collaborationContext field, (2) AI can identify the user with the most overdue ball-in-court items, (3) Suggested prompts appear when user navigates to a RFI or submittal with an idle assignee, (4) Response cites specific names and entity numbers.

issues.title: PresenceBar and EntityPresence components show initials only — no role or status context
issues.description: src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx and src/components/shared/PresenceAvatars.tsx render colored initials circles with no tooltip showing the user's name, role, or last active time. On a 20-person project, a superintendent cannot tell if the person viewing the drawing is the architect or a sub. Violates the Apple-level polish standard.
issues.prompt: In src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, wrap each presence avatar in a Tooltip (from src/components/Primitives.tsx or a Radix Tooltip primitive) that renders: user full name (14px, bold), role from project_members table (12px, muted), last active timestamp formatted as '2 min ago' using a relative time formatter, and a colored status dot (green=active <5min, amber=idle 5-30min, gray=>30min). In src/stores/presenceStore.ts, ensure the presence payload includes userId, displayName, role, and lastSeen. In src/components/shared/PresenceAvatars.tsx, apply the same tooltip pattern. Use 150ms transition on tooltip appear per design standards. Avatar size: 32px circle, 2px white border, overlapping by 8px in a stack. Acceptance criteria: (1) Hovering any presence avatar shows name + role + last active in a tooltip within 150ms, (2) Avatar border color reflects active/idle/away state, (3) More than 5 users collapse into +N badge that shows full list on hover, (4) Tooltip does not overflow viewport on edge positions.

issues.title: NotificationCenter not accessible from mobile bottom navigation
issues.description: src/components/collaboration/NotificationCenter.tsx and src/components/notifications/NotificationCenter.tsx (duplicate) are only reachable via TopBar icon on desktop. On mobile in src/components/layout/MobileLayout.tsx, there is no notification bell in the bottom navigation and no swipe gesture to open the notification panel. Supers on-site miss critical alerts.
issues.prompt: In src/components/layout/MobileLayout.tsx, add a notification bell icon to the bottom navigation bar (5th tab position) with an unread count badge sourced from src/stores/notificationStore.ts unread count. Badge: 18px red circle, white text, positioned top-right of the bell icon, hidden when count is 0. Tapping opens a bottom sheet (slides up 80% of viewport height) containing the NotificationCenter component. In src/components/notifications/NotificationCenter.tsx, deduplicate with src/components/collaboration/NotificationCenter.tsx — keep one canonical file and delete the other, updating all imports. Add swipe-down gesture to dismiss the bottom sheet using a touch handler with 100px threshold. Each notification row must be 64px tall minimum (touch target), show entity type icon, title, relative time, and a right-chevron to navigate to the entity. Swipe-left on a row to mark as read. Acceptance criteria: (1) Notification bell visible in mobile bottom nav with live badge count, (2) Tapping opens bottom sheet with full notification list, (3) Swipe-down dismisses the sheet, (4) Swipe-left on a row marks it read and removes badge decrement, (5) Duplicate NotificationCenter file removed with no broken imports.

issues.title: ConversationThread has no @mention, emoji reaction, or file attachment support
issues.description: src/components/shared/ConversationThread.tsx renders a basic comment list with a text input. There are no @mention completions, no emoji reactions, no file drag-drop, and no reply threading. Procore's RFI thread supports email reply, attachments, and distribution lists. This thread is the primary collaboration surface for RFI back-and-forth.
issues.prompt: In src/components/shared/ConversationThread.tsx, make the following additions: (1) Replace the plain textarea input with the existing MentionInput component from src/components/activity/MentionInput.tsx, passing the current projectId for member autocomplete. (2) Add a file attachment button using the existing UploadZone from src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx rendered in a popover — on file select, upload via src/lib/storage.ts and append the file URL to the message payload. (3) Add emoji reaction row below each message: show existing reactions as pill badges (emoji + count), clicking adds/removes the current user's reaction, stored in a message_reactions table (message_id, user_id, emoji). (4) Add a Reply button that sets a parent_message_id, indenting the reply 24px under the parent with a left border in the primary orange color. (5) Add keyboard shortcut: Cmd+Enter to submit. Acceptance criteria: (1) @mention autocomplete works in thread input, (2) File attachments render as preview cards in the message, (3) Emoji reactions persist across sessions, (4) Reply threading shows visual indent with parent preview, (5) Cmd+Enter submits the message.

issues.title: getAuditLog in auditTrail.ts throws raw Supabase error without wrapping
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts, both getAuditLog and getEntityHistory do `if (error) throw error` instead of `throw transformSupabaseError(error)`. This leaks internal Supabase error shapes (including table names and query details) to the client — a security and enterprise readiness failure. Every other endpoint in the codebase uses transformSupabaseError correctly.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/auditTrail.ts, update both getAuditLog and getEntityHistory to use the standard error transformation pattern already established in the codebase. Import transformSupabaseError from '../client' (it is already re-exported there). Replace `if (error) throw error` with `if (error) throw transformSupabaseError(error)` in both functions. Also add the missing import at the top of the file: `import { transformSupabaseError } from '../client'`. Additionally, add organization-level authorization check at the top of getAuditLog: call validateProjectId(projectId) using the existing middleware from src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts to prevent cross-project audit log access. Acceptance criteria: (1) Both functions import and call transformSupabaseError, (2) validateProjectId is called in getAuditLog before the query executes, (3) No raw Supabase error objects reach the client layer, (4) Existing audit trail tests in src/test/ continue to pass, (5) TypeScript strict mode shows no new errors in this file.

issues.title: PresenceBar and NotificationCenter have no ARIA live regions for real-time updates
issues.description: When a new user joins a session or a notification arrives, there is no ARIA live region announcement. Screen reader users on assistive technology get no alert that the collaboration state has changed. src/components/ui/LiveRegion.tsx exists but is not used in any collaboration component.
issues.prompt: In src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, import LiveRegion from src/components/ui/LiveRegion.tsx. When the presence list changes (a user joins or leaves), announce via LiveRegion with aria-live='polite': '[Name] joined the session' or '[Name] left the session'. Track previous presence list in a useRef to compute the diff. In src/components/notifications/NotificationCenter.tsx, wrap the unread notification count badge in a visually hidden span with role='status' aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' that announces 'You have N unread notifications' when the count changes. In src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, use aria-live='assertive' to announce when an edit lock is acquired by another user (this is urgent context). Ensure the existing src/components/ui/RouteAnnouncer.tsx pattern is followed for consistency. Acceptance criteria: (1) Screen reader announces user presence changes, (2) Notification count changes are announced politely, (3) Edit lock conflicts are announced assertively, (4) LiveRegion component is used rather than custom aria-live divs, (5) No duplicate announcements for the same event.

issues.title: Activity feed returns raw rows with no entity enrichment or grouping
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/activity.ts returns raw activity_feed rows with no join to the entity (RFI title, submittal number, user display name). The feed in src/pages/Activity.tsx shows generic IDs instead of human-readable context. Procore's activity feed shows 'John Smith responded to RFI-042 Curtain Wall Detail' with a link. This is a CFO and PM trust issue.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/activity.ts, update the getActivityFeed query to join related data: add .select('*, user:profiles(id, full_name, avatar_url)') to enrich actor information. In the mapping layer, add a utility function enrichActivityItem(item) that resolves entity_type + entity_id to a display label by querying a client-side cache (using the existing src/hooks/useProjectCache.ts) — for entity_type='rfi' look up the rfi number and subject, for 'submittal' the submittal number and title, for 'change_order' the CO number and description. Export an ActivityFeedItem type in src/types/entities.ts with fields: id, actorName, actorAvatar, verb, entityType, entityLabel, entityId, projectId, createdAt, metadata. In src/pages/Activity.tsx and src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx, render each item as '[Avatar] [ActorName] [verb] [EntityLabel]' with the entity label as a clickable link to the detail view. Group consecutive items from the same actor within 5 minutes. Acceptance criteria: (1) Activity items show actor full name and avatar, (2) Entity references show human-readable labels (e.g. 'RFI-042 Curtain Wall'), (3) Entity labels link to the correct detail page, (4) Consecutive items from same actor are visually grouped, (5) Feed updates in real time via existing Supabase realtime subscription.

issues.title: ProjectMember API uses unsafe any casts in addProjectMember and updateProjectMember
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/projectMembers.ts, addProjectMember and updateProjectMember cast the Supabase query builder to any to bypass TypeScript's type checker. This masks potential schema mismatches and breaks the strict-typing standard. The pattern `(supabase.from('project_members') as any)` is used twice, suppressing errors that could indicate real data contract violations.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/projectMembers.ts, remove both `as any` casts from addProjectMember and updateProjectMember. Instead, extend the Database type in src/types/database.ts to include the project_members table with the correct column definitions: id (string), project_id (string), user_id (string), role (ProjectRole union type), permissions (Json), created_at (string), updated_at (string). Import the ProjectRole type from src/types/tenant.ts and reference it in the database type. After the schema is typed, the Supabase client will infer the correct insert and update shapes without casting. Update the ProjectMember interface in src/types/tenant.ts to match the database schema exactly. Run tsc --noEmit to confirm zero new type errors. Acceptance criteria: (1) No `as any` casts remain in projectMembers.ts, (2) Database type in src/types/database.ts includes project_members table definition, (3) TypeScript infers insert/update payloads correctly without explicit casting, (4) tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors in this file, (5) Existing tests in src/test/permissions.test.ts continue to pass.

invented_features.name: Ball-in-Court Pulse — AI-powered accountability layer
invented_features.description: An AI agent that monitors every RFI, submittal, and change order for ball-in-court status. If a team member has held the ball for more than 48 hours without action, the agent sends a context-aware nudge: 'You have 3 items awaiting your response that are on the critical path. The concrete pour is Thursday.' Nudges are sent via in-app notification, email, and optionally Slack/Teams via the existing integration layer. The PM sees a 'stalled items' panel on the Dashboard showing which team members are blocking the most work, with a one-click escalation button that auto-drafts an escalation message with the AI Copilot.

invented_features.name: Collaborative Drawing Markup with Live Conflict Detection
invented_features.description: Extend the DrawingViewer to support multi-user simultaneous markup with real-time cursor presence (like Figma). When two users markup the same area of a drawing, the AI detects the spatial overlap and surfaces a conflict alert: 'Your markup in Grid C-4 overlaps with Sarah's RFI note. Do you want to link these?' This bridges the gap between field observations and the RFI workflow. Each markup is attributed with the author's name, role, and timestamp, making it legally defensible for dispute resolution.

invented_features.name: Subcontractor External Collaboration Portal
invented_features.description: A lightweight, no-login-required collaboration surface for subcontractors. The GC sends a secure link (tokenized, time-limited) to a sub for a specific RFI response, submittal review, or lien waiver signature. The sub opens the link in any browser, sees only the relevant document with a response form, and submits. Their response flows directly into the SiteSync workflow with full audit trail. This mirrors Procore's email-reply RFI feature but extends it to submittals and lien waivers, eliminating the biggest friction point in GC-subcontractor coordination.

competitive_intel: Procore's collaboration is built around email-reply workflows (architects respond to RFIs without logging in) and a ball-in-court model that the whole industry recognizes. SiteSyncAI's current collaboration module has the infrastructure (presence, realtime, notifications, audit trail) but lacks the domain-specific wiring that makes it useful on a real job site: @mention routing goes nowhere, edit conflicts are UI-only with no backend enforcement, the activity feed shows IDs not context, and the AI has no awareness of who is blocking what. Fieldwire beats SiteSync today on mobile notification delivery for field crews. The invented features above — particularly the external sub portal and ball-in-court AI pulse — would leapfrog Procore's collaboration model if implemented, as Procore has no AI-driven accountability layer and their external access model is limited to RFI email reply only.
summary: The Collaboration & Teamwork module scores 53/100. The foundational infrastructure is solid — Supabase realtime subscriptions, presence stores, audit trail, conflict guards, and notification center all exist as code artifacts. However, the module fails at the domain-specific wiring that makes collaboration meaningful on a construction job site. @mentions produce no notifications, edit locks have no backend enforcement creating real data loss risk, the activity feed returns raw database rows with no human-readable context, and the AI Copilot has zero awareness of team collaboration state. On mobile, the notification center is completely inaccessible from the bottom navigation — meaning supers in the field miss critical alerts. Two duplicate NotificationCenter files indicate architectural drift. The most critical gap versus Procore is the absence of a ball-in-court AI accountability layer: the system stores who owns each action but never proactively escalates stalled items. Fixing the 10 identified issues — particularly the @mention routing, backend edit locks, and mobile notification access — would bring this module to approximately 78/100 and position it ahead of Procore's collaboration model for the first time.
